text.title: Content Audit Types, Scoring, and Governance
text.summary: Audit types, evaluation scoring criteria, frequency recommendations by content type, and post-audit governance workflow.

section.title: Audit Types
section.p: Quantitative — inventory what exists; count and categorize. Qualitative — evaluate quality against defined criteria. Comparative — check consistency across content items. ROI-focused — measure performance using analytics data.

rule.statement: Always cross-reference remove/archive decisions with inbound link data before deletion.
rule.rationale: A low-traffic page with high inbound links should be redirected, not deleted; removing it breaks third-party links and harms SEO.

section.title: Scoring Criteria (1-5 scale)
section.p: Accuracy: 5 = verified recently; 4 = accurate, minor updates needed; 3 = some outdated info; 2 = significant inaccuracies; 1 = largely incorrect. Relevance: 5 = core to user needs; 4 = useful to many users; 3 = useful to some; 2 = limited audience; 1 = no longer relevant. Quality: 5 = excellent, clear, complete; 4 = good, minor issues; 3 = acceptable; 2 = poor or incomplete; 1 = very poor.
Action thresholds by average score: >= 4.0 → Keep; 3.0-3.9 → Update; 2.0-2.9 → Rewrite/consolidate; < 2.0 → Remove/archive.

rule.statement: Define evaluation criteria explicitly before scoring begins; do not score against implicit criteria.
rule.rationale: Subjective quality judgments without written criteria produce inconsistent scores across auditors and audit passes.

section.title: Audit Frequency by Content Type
section.p: Core pages — quarterly. Blog and news — bi-annually. Help and support — quarterly. Product pages — after major releases. Legal and policy — annually.

section.title: Post-Audit Governance Workflow
section.p: New content workflow: Draft → Review → Approve → Publish → schedule review date → track performance → update or archive.

rule.statement: Assign a human owner to each "Update" action item before the audit is considered complete.
rule.rationale: Agent recommendations without human ownership stall; unowned tasks do not get done.

rule.statement: Store the audit in a versioned spreadsheet; keep it for at least one full content cycle.
rule.rationale: Version history enables tracking governance effectiveness and provides baseline for the next audit pass.

text.reference: Full column definitions for the content audit spreadsheet
